_metadata.description: G.I. Joe: A Real American Hero (1982-1994) — G.I. Joe team characters. All named characters who received action figures in the ARAH 3.75" line.

characters.name: Breaker
characters.notes: Real name: Alvin R. Kibbey. Communications Officer. One of the Original 13 Joes. First release 1982.

characters.name: Clutch
characters.notes: Real name: Lance J. Steinberg. Transportation/VAMP Driver. One of the Original 13 Joes. First release 1982.

characters.name: Flash
characters.notes: Real name: Anthony S. Gambello. Laser Rifle Trooper. One of the Original 13 Joes. First release 1982.

characters.name: Grand Slam
characters.notes: Real name: James J. Barney. Laser Artillery Soldier/HAL driver. One of the Original 13 Joes. First release 1982.

characters.name: Grunt
characters.notes: Real name: Robert W. Graves. Infantry. One of the Original 13 Joes. First release 1982.

characters.name: Hawk
characters.notes: Real name: Clayton M. Abernathy. Commander/Artillery. G.I. Joe team leader. One of the Original 13 Joes. First release 1982.

characters.name: Rock 'n Roll
characters.notes: Real name: Craig S. McConnel. Machine Gunner. One of the Original 13 Joes. First release 1982.

characters.name: Scarlett
characters.notes: Real name: Shana M. O'Hara. Counter Intelligence. First female Joe. One of the Original 13 Joes. First release 1982.

characters.name: Short-Fuze
characters.notes: Real name: Eric W. Freistadt. Mortar Soldier. One of the Original 13 Joes. First release 1982.

characters.name: Snake Eyes
characters.notes: Real name: CLASSIFIED. Commando/Ninja. Most popular Joe character. Mute due to helicopter crash injuries. One of the Original 13 Joes. First release 1982.

characters.name: Stalker
characters.notes: Real name: Lonzo R. Wilkinson. Ranger. One of the Original 13 Joes. First release 1982.

characters.name: Steeler
characters.notes: Real name: Ralph W. Pulaski. MOBAT Tank Driver. One of the Original 13 Joes. First release 1982.

characters.name: Zap
characters.notes: Real name: Rafael J. Melendez. Bazooka Soldier. One of the Original 13 Joes. First release 1982.

characters.name: Ace
characters.notes: Real name: Brad J. Armbruster. Fighter Pilot (Skystriker). First release 1983.

characters.name: Airborne
characters.notes: Real name: Franklin E. Talltree. Helicopter Assault Trooper. First release 1983.

characters.name: Cover Girl
characters.notes: Real name: Courtney A. Krieger. Tank Driver/Wolverine Driver. Former fashion model. First release 1983.

characters.name: Doc
characters.notes: Real name: Carl W. Greer. Medic. First release 1983.

characters.name: Duke
characters.notes: Real name: Conrad S. Hauser. Field Commander/First Sergeant. Face of the G.I. Joe team in media. Mail-in 1983, carded 1984.

characters.name: Gung-Ho
characters.notes: Real name: Ettienne R. LaFitte. Marine/Recondo. Cajun. Distinctive chest tattoo. First release 1983.

characters.name: Snow Job
characters.notes: Real name: Harlan W. Moore. Arctic Trooper. Olympic biathlete. First release 1983.

characters.name: Torpedo
characters.notes: Real name: Edward W. Leialoha. Navy SEAL. First release 1983.

characters.name: Tripwire
characters.notes: Real name: Tormod S. Skoog. Mine Detector/EOD. Notoriously clumsy. First release 1983.

characters.name: Wild Bill
characters.notes: Real name: William S. Hardy. Helicopter Pilot (Dragonfly). Texan cowboy. First release 1983.

characters.name: Blowtorch
characters.notes: Real name: Timothy P. Hanrahan. Flamethrower. First release 1984.

characters.name: Cutter
characters.notes: Real name: Ronald A. Stone. Hovercraft Pilot (Whale). Coast Guard. First release 1984.

characters.name: Deep Six
characters.notes: Real name: Malcolm R. Willoughby. Deep Sea Diver (SHARC pilot). First release 1984.

characters.name: Mutt
characters.notes: Real name: Stanley R. Perlmutter. Dog Handler (with Junkyard the dog). First release 1984.

characters.name: Recondo
characters.notes: Real name: Daniel M. LeClaire. Jungle Trooper. First release 1984.

characters.name: Rip Cord
characters.notes: Real name: Wallace A. Weems. HALO Jumper. First release 1984.

characters.name: Roadblock
characters.notes: Real name: Marvin F. Hinton. Heavy Machine Gunner. Gourmet chef. Speaks in rhyme. First release 1984.

characters.name: Spirit
characters.notes: Real name: Charlie Iron-Knife. Tracker (with Freedom the eagle). Native American. First release 1984.

characters.name: Thunder
characters.notes: Real name: Matthew H. Breckinridge. Self-Propelled Gun (Slugger) Driver. First release 1984.

characters.name: Airtight
characters.notes: Real name: Kurt Schnurr. Hostile Environment/CBR Specialist. First release 1985.

characters.name: Alpine
characters.notes: Real name: Albert M. Pine. Mountain Trooper. First release 1985.

characters.name: Barbecue
characters.notes: Real name: Gabriel A. Kelly. Fire Fighter. First release 1985.

characters.name: Bazooka
characters.notes: Real name: David L. Katzenbogen. Missile Specialist. Wears iconic football jersey. First release 1985.

characters.name: Crankcase
characters.notes: Real name: Elwood G. Indiana. AWE Striker Driver. First release 1985.

characters.name: Dusty
characters.notes: Real name: Ronald W. Tadur. Desert Trooper. First release 1985.

characters.name: Flint
characters.notes: Real name: Dashiell R. Faireborn. Warrant Officer. Rhodes Scholar. Lady Jaye's love interest. First release 1985.

characters.name: Footloose
characters.notes: Real name: Andrew D. Meyers. Infantry. First release 1985.

characters.name: Frostbite
characters.notes: Real name: Farley S. Seward. Snow Cat Driver. From Galena, Alaska. First release 1985.

characters.name: Heavy Metal
characters.notes: Real name: Sherman R. Guderian. Mauler MBT Tank Driver. First release 1985.

characters.name: Keel-Haul
characters.notes: Real name: Everett P. Colby. Admiral/USS Flagg Commander. First release 1985.

characters.name: Lady Jaye
characters.notes: Real name: Alison R. Hart-Burnett. Covert Operations. Flint's love interest. First release 1985.

characters.name: Quick Kick
characters.notes: Real name: MacArthur S. Ito. Silent Weapons/Martial Arts. Hollywood stuntman. First release 1985.

characters.name: Shipwreck
characters.notes: Real name: Hector X. Delgado. Sailor (with Polly the parrot). Fan-favorite cartoon character. First release 1985.

characters.name: Sgt. Slaughter
characters.notes: Real name: Robert Remus. Drill Instructor. Based on real WWF pro wrestler. Mail-in 1985, carded 1986.

characters.name: Starduster
characters.notes: Real name: Edward J. Skylar. Jet Pack Trooper. Mail-in exclusive. First release 1985.

characters.name: Tollbooth
characters.notes: Real name: Chuck X. Goren. Bridge Layer (Toss 'N Cross) Driver. First release 1985.

characters.name: Beach Head
characters.notes: Real name: Wayne R. Sneeden. Ranger. Known for his balaclava and tough demeanor. First release 1986.

characters.name: Claymore
characters.notes: Real name: John Zullo. Anti-Terrorist Specialist. Special Mission: Brazil TRU exclusive. First release 1986.

characters.name: Cross-Country
characters.notes: Real name: Robert M. Blais. H.A.V.O.C. Driver. Country music fan. First release 1986.

characters.name: Dial-Tone
characters.notes: Real name: Jack S. Morelli. Communications. First release 1986.

characters.name: Iceberg
characters.notes: Real name: Clifton L. Nash. Arctic Trooper. From Brownsville, Texas. First release 1986.

characters.name: Leatherneck
characters.notes: Real name: Wendell A. Metzger. Marine Gunner. First release 1986.

characters.name: Lifeline
characters.notes: Real name: Edwin C. Steen. Rescue Trooper/Medic. Pacifist who refuses to carry weapons. First release 1986.

characters.name: Lift-Ticket
characters.notes: Real name: Victor W. Sikorski. Helicopter Pilot (Tomahawk). First release 1986.

characters.name: Low-Light
characters.notes: Real name: Cooper G. MacBride. Night Spotter. Dark childhood backstory. First release 1986.

characters.name: Mainframe
characters.notes: Real name: Blaine L. Parker. Computer Specialist. First release 1986.

characters.name: Sci-Fi
characters.notes: Real name: Seymour P. Fine. Laser Trooper. Distinctive green armor. First release 1986.

characters.name: Slip-Stream
characters.notes: Real name: Gregory B. Boyajian. Conquest X-30 Fighter Pilot. First release 1986.

characters.name: Wet-Suit
characters.notes: Real name: Brian M. Forrest. Navy SEAL. Rival of Leatherneck. First release 1986.

characters.name: Avalanche
characters.notes: Real name: Ian M. Costello. Battle Force 2000 Snow Vehicle Driver. First release 1987.

characters.name: Backstop
characters.notes: Real name: Robert A. Levin. Persuader Driver. Canadian. First release 1987.

characters.name: Blaster
characters.notes: Real name: Brian R. Davis. Battle Force 2000 Vindicator Pilot. First release 1987.

characters.name: Blocker
characters.notes: Real name: David B. McCarthy. Battle Force 2000 Eliminator Driver. First release 1987.

characters.name: Chuckles
characters.notes: Real name: Philip M. Provost. Undercover Operative. Hawaiian shirt disguise. First release 1987.

characters.name: Crazylegs
characters.notes: Real name: David O. Thomas. Assault Trooper/Paratrooper. First release 1987.

characters.name: Dodger
characters.notes: Real name: Richard Renwick. Battle Force 2000 Marauder Driver. First release 1987.

characters.name: Falcon
characters.notes: Real name: Vincent R. Falcone. Green Beret. Duke's half-brother. Featured in G.I. Joe: The Movie. First release 1987.

characters.name: Fast Draw
characters.notes: Real name: Eliot Brown. Mobile Missile Specialist. First release 1987.

characters.name: Hardtop
characters.notes: Real name: Nicholas D. Klas. Defiant Shuttle Complex Driver. First release 1987.

characters.name: Jinx
characters.notes: Real name: CLASSIFIED (Kim Arashikage in some sources). Ninja Intelligence. Arashikage clan. First release 1987.

characters.name: Knockdown
characters.notes: Real name: Blaine M. Gonsalves. Battle Force 2000 Sky Sweeper Driver. First release 1987.

characters.name: Law & Order
characters.notes: Real name: Christopher M. Lavigne. Military Policeman (with Order the dog). First release 1987.

characters.name: Maverick
characters.notes: Real name: Thomas P. Kiley. Battle Force 2000 Fighter Pilot. First release 1987.

characters.name: Mercer

characters.name: Payload

characters.name: Psyche-Out

characters.name: Rumbler

characters.name: Steel Brigade

characters.notes: Real name: Varujan Ayvazyan. Sgt. Slaughter's Renegades. Former circus acrobat. First release 1987.

characters.name: Action Marine
characters.notes: 30th Anniversary commemorative figure based on the original 1964 G.I. Joe. Marine. First release 1994.
